evidence_index.verbatim_quote: In February 2021, Robert Damiani (hereinafter the decedent) was brought to the emergency department of St. Joseph Hospital (hereinafter the hospital) with complaints of dehydration and tachycardia after having tested positive for COVID-19.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Decedent admitted to hospital with dehydration and tachycardia after COVID-19 positive test.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Three days after his intubation, a nurse noted a sacral pressure ulcer, which was measured and treated with topical medication.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Pressure ulcer noted and treated three days post-intubation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The note stated that the decedent was to be turned and repositioned every two hours, and a later note indicated that the 'Hyperbaric Wound Care Center' should be notified and a wound care evaluation performed.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Decedent to be repositioned every two hours; wound care evaluation recommended.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The defendants failed to conclusively demonstrate that, at a minimum, once the pressure ulcer developed, the alleged negligent acts and omissions relating to the treatment of that condition occurred in the course of providing treatment for COVID-19 or care to an individual with a confirmed case of COVID-19 'as it relates to COVID-19'.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Defendants did not prove pressure ulcer care was related to COVID-19 treatment.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The defendants' evidentiary submissions 'failed to establish that there is no significant dispute as to whether the [decedent's] pressure ulcer[ ] arose from the use of a ventilator,' such that the defendants failed to demonstrate their entitlement to dismissal of the complaint.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Defendants failed to show pressure ulcer was ventilator-related.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: The defendants' position that since the decedent was being treated for COVID-19, all the care that he received in the hospital, including for a pressure ulcer, was related to COVID-19 would not give any effect to the amendment.
evidence_index.paragraph_starts_with: In an action, inter alia, to
evidence_index.summary: Court rejects defendants' claim that all care was COVID-19 related.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

initial_state.chief_complaint: Dehydration and tachycardia after testing positive for COVID-19
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented with dehydration and tachycardia following a positive COVID-19 test.

vital_signs.heart_rate: Tachycardic per judgment, exact rate not stated

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Dehydration and tachycardia in the context of COVID-19

requestables.name: Pressure Ulcer Care
requestables.description: Nursing notes on pressure ulcer care and repositioning

reveal.result_summary: Pressure ulcer noted and treated with topical medication; repositioning every two hours recommended.
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates development of pressure ulcer and care plan

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient intubated and developing pressure ulcer
decision_points.prompt: What should be done to manage the pressure ulcer?

options.description: Initiate wound care evaluation and reposition every two hours
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care for pressure ulcer management

options.description: Continue current care without additional wound care evaluation
options.clinical_reasoning: Assumed all care was related to COVID-19

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant continued current care without additional wound care evaluation
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant assumed all care was related to COVID-19

expected_action_court.description: Wound care evaluation should have been initiated
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Court found failure to address pressure ulcer appropriately

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Defendants failed to demonstrate pressure ulcer care was related to COVID-19 treatment
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Wound care evaluation and repositioning should have been prioritized
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Court rejected the claim that all care was COVID-19 related

key_events.event: Patient admitted with dehydration and tachycardia

timeline_phases.name: Post-Intubation Care
timeline_phases.description: Care following intubation, including pressure ulcer management

patient_outcome.description: Development of sacral pressure ulcer requiring wound care

key_findings: Defendants failed to demonstrate pressure ulcer care was related to COVID-19 treatment
Failure to initiate appropriate wound care evaluation

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to initiate wound care evaluation
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Appropriate wound care could have mitigated ulcer progression

factual_timeline.event: Patient admitted to hospital with dehydration and tachycardia

allegations.allegation: Failure to initiate wound care evaluation

court_findings.finding: Defendants failed to demonstrate pressure ulcer care was related to COVID-19 treatment
court_findings.legal_significance: Liability found for inadequate pressure ulcer management
